metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4785
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 4, S4: "Overexpression of the ANK3 (ankyrin-G) 190 kDa isoform, GFPAnkG190, caused a 24% increase in spine area compared with control, contributed by both an increase in spine length and head width and no effect on spine density (Figure S4C), suggesting that at this mature time point, ankyrin-G190 is primarily modulating the maintenance of existing spines."

Figure 5, S5: 190 kDa Ankyrin-G modulates spine head and neck width, based on SIM Imaging.
"We analyzed neurons over-expressing GFPAnkG190 and found that the presence of GFPAnkG190 anywhere in the spine causes enlargement of head and neck width in mushroom spines compared to spines without GFPAnkG190 or spines from neurons expressing mCherry alone. This effect was specific to mushroom-type spines and not thin spines, although the overexpression of GFPAnkG190 caused no changes in the abundance of mushroom spines". Experimental description: Supplemental M&M: "Dissociated cultures of primary cortical neurons were prepared from E18 Sprague-Dawley rat embryos."

antibodies:
Ankyrin-G mAb (Neuromab)
Ankyrin-G pAb (Santa Cruz)
Ankyrin-G pAb (from V. Bennett)

"GFP-Ankyrin-G-190 and RFP-Ankyrin-G-270 were purchased from Addgene (#31059, #42566). RNAi constructs were purchased from Origene, in the pGFP-V-RS vector with a turboGFP element to enable identification of transfected cells. The target sequence used was GGCAGAACGAGACGCCAAGTGGAAGCCTA"
